title: Diagrammatic Monte Carlo for Fermionic Rényi Entanglement Entropy
abstract: We develop a direct diagrammatic Monte Carlo framework for the Renyi entanglement entropy of interacting lattice fermions. The method starts from the fermionic graded-swap representation of Z_n[A]=Tr_Aρ_A^n, which converts the entropy problem into a replicated path integral with mixed temporal boundary conditions on the entangling region. In this representation the replica momenta are half-shifted, q_m=(2m+1)π/n, and the interaction expansion has a determinant form suitable for connected-determinant summation. We combine this expansion with a many-configuration Markov-chain Monte Carlo sampler to obtain order-by-order corrections for very large systems to very high orders. As a benchmark, we compare the order-by-order coefficients of a 3*3 Hubbard cluster with exact diagonalization. We then report a production calculation for a large periodic lattice with a square subregions. The dominant system-size limitation is therefore memory rather than a conventional auxiliary-field sign problem. The results provide a step toward diagrammatic calculations of fermionic entanglement observables in regimes where direct quantum Monte Carlo sampling is costly or sign-problem limited.
summary: This paper introduces a new Diagrammatic Monte Carlo method to directly compute Renyi entanglement entropy in interacting fermionic systems. By using a graded-swap representation and connected-determinant sampling, the method avoids the traditional sign problem and scales to much larger lattice sizes than exact diagonalization. This provides a powerful tool for studying entanglement in complex, strongly correlated regimes.
main_problem: Developing a scalable numerical framework to calculate the Renyi entanglement entropy for interacting lattice fermions, specifically overcoming the sign problem and computational costs in 2D and finite-temperature regimes.
main_result: The authors successfully implemented a Diagrammatic Monte Carlo (DiagMC) framework that provides accurate order-by-order interaction corrections for large systems, validated against exact diagonalization for small clusters and applied to a 40x40 lattice.
method: A direct diagrammatic Monte Carlo framework using a fermionic graded-swap representation, connected-determinant sampling (CDet), and a many-configuration Markov-chain Monte Carlo sampler.
model_or_system: The repulsive Hubbard model on a square lattice with periodic boundary conditions, focusing on the entanglement of a square subregion A.
key_observables: Second Renyi entanglement entropy (S2) and its interaction-dependent coefficients.
important_parameters: Interaction strength (U), chemical potential (mu), inverse temperature (beta), and subregion size/geometry.
main_assumptions: The primary computational bottleneck is memory usage rather than the conventional auxiliary-field sign problem; the method is oriented toward finite-size/finite-region calculations.
figures_summary: Figure 1 compares order-by-order CDet Monte Carlo coefficients against exact diagonalization for a 3x3 cluster and presents production results for a 40x40 lattice.
paper_structure: The paper follows a logical progression from the theoretical formulation of the graded-swap path integral to the derivation of the determinant expansion, followed by the description of the MCMC sampler, validation via benchmark, and finally large-scale production results.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Order-by-order interaction corrections. Left: 3 × 3 benchmark with ED derivatives, shown as red stars, for the first three coefficients. Right: 40 × 40 production run for a periodic origin-square subregion with radius r = 8. The plotted values are coefficients of ln Z2[A]/N, not cumulative sums; the omitted zeroth-order terms are the free-system values −0.27507894 (left) and −0.04089329 (right).
